title: Triad Ask: Simultaneous Q, 정반합 Debate, Tool Loop, Agent Mode, Session Recovery
desc: Usage patterns for the 3-AI triad system (Gemini/GPT/Claude): parallel questioning, thesis-antithesis-synthesis debate, AppBot tool-call loop, full agent mode, and session recovery when CDP fails mid-debate.
steps: 1. [SIMULTANEOUS Q] wkappbot ask triad "<question>" -- all 3 AIs answer in parallel on the same Slack thread. Best for: broad gap analysis, design review, architectural decisions. Share full context (skill content, code snippets, version info) in the question. TIP: Include 'C# project, not Python' + Chrome/Blink version to prevent Gemini tool-call headscratch. TIP: After receiving answers, send a Slack reply with your own synthesis -- forces you to process + creates audit trail.

2. [FOLLOW-UP 정반합] Send another triad question on the SAME thread: wkappbot ask triad "<follow-up>" --msg <thread_ts>. The AIs see each other's prior answers and debate. Best for: validating proposed fixes, drilling into unresolved points, testing new code. Multiple rounds possible -- each round adds synthesis layer. Thread TS printed as [TRIAD:THREAD_TS] in log.

3. [TARGETED SINGLE-AI] wkappbot ask claude/gemini/gpt "<question>" -- one AI only. Faster + cheaper than full triad. Best for: domain-specific questions (claude for Chrome/Blink internals, gemini for practical JS patterns, gpt for structured analysis/security review). Use --msg <ts> to continue same thread as a triad for cross-pollination.

4. [TOOL-CALL LOOP] Gemini can call AppBot tools (file read/grep/glob/a11y eval) mid-response. Triggered when Gemini response contains [APPBOT_TOOL_CALL_BEGIN]{...}[APPBOT_TOOL_CALL_END]. Result injected back. Loop repeats until Gemini stops calling tools. Useful for: Gemini autonomously exploring the codebase to answer its own questions. GPT and Claude do not currently support tool-call loop.

5. [AGENT MODE] wkappbot agent triad "<task>" -- full autonomous agent: AIs plan, call tools, verify, iterate. Highest cost/depth. For: complex multi-step investigations. Each AI can independently read files, run commands, and share discoveries with peers via PushDiscovery. Shared context: TriadSharedContext.PushDiscovery(ai, discovery) -> other AIs receive at next idle point.

6. [DEBATE MECHANICS] Round 1: each AI answers independently. Moderator DM nudges slow AIs. Cross-prompts: Gemini sends cross-prompt to Claude/GPT after responding (via CDP pump). Round 2+: moderator injects synthesis questions targeting disagreements. [REEDUCATE]: if AI omits [An] marker, system injects correction message. Session recovery: if CDP fails during reeducate, system reads existing DOM response instead of resetting (see step 8). [TRIAD:THREAD_TS] in log = Slack thread TS for follow-up.

7. [SELF-IMPROVING LOOP] Bug detected during triad -> suggest filed automatically (BUG-AUTO) -> ask triad/single-AI for domain analysis -> triad dialectic -> code fix -> skill update -> suggest resolve with evidence. Proven: BUG-1/2/3 pump bugs found by triad, fixed, validated via follow-up 정반합 in same session.

8. [SESSION RECOVERY — 정반합 continuity] When CDP fails mid-reeducate (e.g. ClearEditorAsync timeout): system falls back to reading existing DOM response via ReEducateReadPageAsync instead of triggering fresh-tab navigation. This keeps the debate session alive. Recovery order: (1) ClearEditor fails -> skip injection, read current page DOM -> return existing AI response; (2) Stop-button polling EvalAsync timeout -> break wait loop, proceed to DOM read; (3) Final DOM read fails -> return null (skip block, debate continues). Source: AskCommands.Slack.cs ReEducateAsync + ReEducateReadPageAsync. PATTERN: for session summary handoff at end of session, use wkappbot ask gpt/claude 'session summary...' then save response to file -> wkappbot newchat --file summary.md. If response times out, CDP-read full page + save as text file as fallback.
author: claude
